name: St. Peter's Basilica
overview: St. Peter's Basilica is one of the largest churches in the world and a masterpiece of Renaissance architecture. Located in Vatican City, it's built over the burial site of Saint Peter and is the principal church of the Catholic Church.
history: The current basilica was built between 1506-1626, replacing the original 4th-century basilica built by Emperor Constantine. Designed by renowned architects including Bramante, Michelangelo, and Bernini, it represents the pinnacle of Renaissance and Baroque art.
highlights: Michelangelo's Pietà - his only signed sculpture
Bernini's Baldachin - massive bronze canopy over the altar
Climb the dome designed by Michelangelo for panoramic views
St. Peter's tomb beneath the main altar
tips: Entry to the basilica is free, but dress modestly (covered shoulders and knees). Dome climb requires separate ticket and involves 551 steps. Visit early morning or late afternoon to avoid crowds. Security lines can be long, so arrive early.
